title: Brachystelma tuberosum
taxonomy: Brachystelma tuberosum is classified within the plant family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane family. This classification places it among other flowering plants that are native to southern Africa. Within the Apocynaceae family, Brachystelma tuberosum is part of the tribe Staplieae, which comprises various succulent and tuberous species. The genus Brachystelma is a small group of succulent plants, mostly native to southern Africa, and is closely related to the genus Stapelia. The classification of Brachystelma tuberosum is further defined by its unique characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Brachystelma tuberosum, also known as the "tubered Brachystelma," is a flowering plant species in the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its unique, tuberous roots and delicate white or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
